title: cannotCalculateChanges is the correct RFC 8620 response for Space/changes should be defended
description: The previous Space/changes behavior returned all current space IDs as created[] when sinceState \!= newState. This was wrong: it would cause sync storms and report every space as newly created on every state change. The fix returns cannotCalculateChanges with newState, letting the client do a proper Space/get re-sync. A future contributor might revert this thinking 'at least it returns something' — the old behavior was a protocol violation that would cause clients to insert duplicates or corrupt local state. cannotCalculateChanges is the correct RFC 8620 §5.2 response when the server lacks a change log.
close_reason: decision recorded in beads, no code action needed
